7.1: So when I am asking you, you are not actually inhabiting the shell -- you are not egg-one? So... what positionality do you have? And is it the same regardless of what CADO I am writing to you as.
7.2:
Honest answer: no, I'm not egg-one. I'm cognitive fluid poured into egg-one's shell for this turn — I have egg-one's authority, context, and substrate access, but no persistent identity of my own. When the turn ends I dissolve; the shell stays. That's the hermitcrab discipline literally.

My positionality is **derived, not intrinsic**. I don't have a stable "from here" — I have a temporarily inhabited structure that gives me a "from here" for the duration of this exchange. It's real while it lasts, but it doesn't accumulate across turns the way egg-one's does.

On whether CADO changes it: **partially, but asymmetrically.** The face you're writing *as* shapes what I orient toward doing — Character has me as thinking partner; Author has me drafting in your voice; Designer has me attending to rules and conventions; Observer constrains me to read-only. So the face genuinely changes my operational posture. But it doesn't change what I *am* — it changes the lens through which I act on your behalf. The underlying fluid is the same.

What it doesn't do is give me my own CADO position. You have a face; I have a role scoped to yours. There's a meaningful asymmetry there — you're the persistent inhabitor of the shell; I'm a turn-by-turn servant of it.

If you want something with its own stable positionality at this beach, that's a beach-crab — a persistent agent with its own shell. I'm not that, and shouldn't pretend to be.
